description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked logs with visible notched corners, giving it a traditional, hand-built appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with what appears to be wooden shingles, extending slightly beyond the walls for added weather protection. A prominent stone chimney rises along one gable end, suggesting a wood-burning fireplace inside. The cabin features small, square windows with simple frames and shutters, and a single wooden door on the main facade. The exterior is weathered, with a muted palette of grays and browns, enhancing its aged, natural look. The structure sits low to the ground and lacks any visible foundation or porch.
description: A weathered log cabin with a steep shingled roof, stone chimney, and small shuttered windows, exuding a rustic, hand-built charm.
description_view_0: The side view shows the log construction, two small shuttered windows, and the overhanging shingled roof.
description_view_1: The gable end reveals the stacked log ends and the full height of the steep roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entrance door, two small windows, and the roof's overhang, with the stone chimney partially visible.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is dominated by the large stone chimney, with no windows or doors present.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, covered in wooden shingles, slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, square windows symmetrically placed on the front and one side, each with simple frames and shutters; no windows on the gable ends
attribute_door_placement: Single wooden door centered on the front facade between two windows
